Historically, the use of the various term "foremost model" has been used to group together the operational and non-operational models within a logical model. Foremost models try to provide the most accurate and logical explanation for how an organization is likely to perform in the given context. In a more recent time, "most operational model" has come to refer to an operating model which is capable of performing the most accurate and logical analysis. Some may also use the term "most fundamental model". This groupings together the operational and non-operational models within a logical model to better understand systems and challenges more generally.
